description_long: This object is a vintage wall-mounted corded telephone, reminiscent of early 20th-century designs. The main body is constructed from dark, polished wood with a rectangular backplate and a protruding front section that houses two metallic bells and a circular mouthpiece. A black cylindrical handset, connected by a fabric-covered cord, hangs on a metal hook to the left side of the unit. The front panel features a metallic speaker grille, a set of push buttons, and a decorative nameplate. Brass or bronze accents are visible on the bells, mouthpiece, and hardware, adding to its antique appearance. The overall design suggests durability and a sense of historical craftsmanship, suitable for period urban environments.
description: A vintage wall-mounted corded telephone with a wooden body, brass accents, and a black cylindrical handset attached by a cord. The front features metallic bells, a circular mouthpiece, and a set of push buttons.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the wooden body, metallic bells, circular mouthpiece, push buttons, and the black handset hanging on the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the wooden case, the protruding mouthpiece, and the profile of the bells.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the flat wooden backplate and the cord looping around the side, with minimal visible hardware.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handset, cord, and the side profile of the mouthpiece and bells.
